title: Data type awareness
summary: Understand how SQL data types affect inserting data, why explicit column lists matter, and how to avoid common mistakes with types in the inventory_items table.

sketchBlocks.title: Why data types matter in INSERT
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown: When inserting data into a table like `inventory_items`, each column expects a specific data type. For example, `price` expects a REAL (number), `name` expects TEXT, and `is_test` expects an INTEGER (usually 0 or 1). If you try to insert a string into `price`, or leave out a NOT NULL column, the database will raise an error or insert incorrect data. Always check the table schema before inserting.

sketchBlocks.title: Safer INSERT with explicit columns
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown:
It's best practice to specify column names in your INSERT statement. For example:

```sql
INSERT INTO inventory_items (name, category, price, status, is_test, last_updated)
VALUES ('New Magnet', 'Accessories', 3.50, 'active', 0, '2026-02-10');
```

This ensures the values match the correct columns and data types, even if the table structure changes later.

sketchBlocks.title: NULLs and defaults in INSERT
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown: Some columns allow NULL values or have default values. For example, `notes` can be NULL, and `status` defaults to 'draft' if not provided. If you omit a column with a default, the default is used. If you omit a NOT NULL column without a default, the INSERT will fail.

quizDraft.title: Insert a new inventory item with correct data types
quizDraft.prompt:
Write an INSERT statement to add a new item to the `inventory_items` table with the following values:
- name: 'Desk Organizer'
- category: 'Accessories'
- price: 8.75
- status: 'active'
- is_test: 0
- notes: NULL
- last_updated: '2026-02-10'

Use an explicit column list and do not provide a value for `id` (let it auto-increment).
quizDraft.hint: List all columns except id in the INSERT statement. Use NULL for notes.

help.concept: When inserting data, match each value to its column and data type. Use explicit columns to avoid mistakes.
help.hint_2: Remember to use NULL for the notes column and provide all other required values.

quizDraft.solutionCode:
INSERT INTO inventory_items (name, category, price, status, is_test, notes, last_updated)
VALUES ('Desk Organizer', 'Accessories', 8.75, 'active', 0, NULL, '2026-02-10');

quizDraft.title: Insert with missing NOT NULL column
quizDraft.prompt: Try to insert a new row into `inventory_items` with only the columns: name, category, and price. What happens and why?
quizDraft.hint: Some columns are NOT NULL and have no default.

help.concept: NOT NULL columns require a value unless a default is provided. Omitting them causes an error.
help.hint_1: Check which columns in inventory_items are NOT NULL and have no default.
help.hint_2: If you omit such a column, the database will reject the insert.

quizDraft.solutionCode:
-- This will fail because status, is_test, and last_updated are NOT NULL and not provided.
-- Example of a correct insert (for learning):
-- INSERT INTO inventory_items (name, category, price, status, is_test, last_updated)
-- VALUES ('Mini Lamp', 'Decor', 14.99, 'active', 0, '2026-02-11');

quizDraft.title: Insert using default values
quizDraft.prompt:
Insert a new item into `inventory_items` with the following values:
- name: 'Sample Keychain'
- category: 'Accessories'
- price: 2.50
- is_test: 1
- last_updated: '2026-02-12'

Omit the `status` and `notes` columns so that defaults and NULLs are used where appropriate.
quizDraft.hint: Omit status and notes from the column list. The table will use the default for status and NULL for notes.

help.concept: If a column has a default value or allows NULL, you can omit it in the INSERT statement.
help.hint_1: Write only the columns you want to provide. The rest will use defaults or NULL.
help.hint_2: status will default to 'draft', notes will be NULL.

quizDraft.solutionCode:
INSERT INTO inventory_items (name, category, price, is_test, last_updated)
VALUES ('Sample Keychain', 'Accessories', 2.50, 1, '2026-02-12');

quizDraft.title: Which columns can be omitted in an INSERT?
quizDraft.prompt: Which of the following columns in `inventory_items` can you safely omit from an INSERT statement (and still have the row inserted successfully)? Select all that apply.
quizDraft.hint: Look for columns that have a default value or allow NULL.

help.concept: Columns with a default value or that allow NULL can be omitted in an INSERT. NOT NULL columns without a default must be included.
help.hint_1: Check the schema for columns with DEFAULT or NULL allowed.
help.hint_2: status, notes, and id can be omitted; others usually require a value.

quizDraft.title: Default value for status
quizDraft.prompt: What is the default value for the `status` column in `inventory_items` if you omit it in an INSERT?
quizDraft.hint: Check the schema for the DEFAULT keyword on status.

help.concept: A column with a DEFAULT value will use that value if not provided in an INSERT.
help.hint_1: Look for the DEFAULT value in the CREATE TABLE statement for status.
help.hint_2: The default is a string value set in the schema.

quizDraft.template: The default value for status is: ___
